name: Montserrat Monastery and Santa Cova Loop from Collbató
name_original: Collbató, Camí de la Font Seca, Montserrat, Santa Cova – Ruta Circular
description: This difficult 8.6-mile (13.8 km) loop takes you through Montserrat Natural Park, visiting Montserrat Monastery and the Holy Cave of Montser
region_intro: Hiking around Collbató offers access to the dramatic landscapes of the Montserrat mountain range. The region is characterized by its distinctive conglomerate rock formations, dense Mediterranean forests, and varied terrain. These features provide a diverse environment for outdoor activities, ranging from gentle forest paths to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Collbató The most popular hiking route is Montserrat: from the Monastery to Sant Jeroni – Circular route, a 6.6 miles (10.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate circular path offers panoramic views of the monastery and the surrounding peaks. Another top favourite among local hikers is Montserrat Monastery and Santa Cova Loop from Collbató, a difficult 8.6 miles (13.8 km) path. This route explores the Montserrat Monastery area and includes a visit to the Santa Cova grotto, with significant elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Sant Miquel Hermitage – View of the Montserrat Monastery loop from Santa Cova, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through rocky landscapes with views of Montserrat Monastery, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Collbató is defined by its unique rock formations, forested slopes, and historical sites like the Montserrat Monastery. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from moderate walks to more advanced ascents. The routes in Collbató are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1,200 reviews. More than 9,800 hikers have used komoot to explore Collbató's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Collbató?
A: Collbató offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 65 routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging ascents through the Montserrat mountain range.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Collbató?
A: Yes, Collbató has 16 easy hiking trails. These routes are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them perfect for a relaxed outing. You can find options that explore the lower slopes and forests around the village.

Q: What are some challenging hiking routes in Collbató?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Collbató offers 12 difficult routes. One notable difficult path is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e947793118/montserrat-monastery-and-santa-cova-loop-from-collbato" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montserrat Monastery and Santa Cova Loop from Collbató&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 8.6 miles (13.8 km) with significant elevation changes as it explores the monastery area and the Santa Cova grotto.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Collbató?
A: Many of the trails around Collbató are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e945461356/montserrat-from-the-monastery-to-sant-jeroni-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montserrat: from the Monastery to Sant Jeroni – Circular route&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views and returns to its starting point.

Q: What kind of viewpoints can I expect on the hiking trails?
A: The trails around Collbató, especially those leading into Montserrat, offer spectacular viewpoints. You can expect panoramic vistas of the unique conglomerate rock formations, the monastery, and the surrounding plains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/293531" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermits' Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a popular spot for breathtaking scenery.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking in Collbató provides access to several significant landmarks. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/238697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montserrat Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, explore various hermitages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558651" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Onofre Hermitage&lt;/a&gt;, and even reach the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151875" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Jeroni Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point of Montserrat.

Q: Is Collbató accessible by public transport for hikers?
A: Yes, Collbató is generally accessible by public transport, making it convenient for hikers. You can typically reach Collbató by bus from nearby towns or train stations, which then allows you to start your hikes directly from the village.

Q: Where can I park my car when hiking in Collbató?
A: Collbató offers various parking options, particularly near the village center or trailheads leading into the Montserrat Natural Park. Specific parking availability can vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Collbató?
A: The best time to hike in Collbató is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for ideal hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and winters, while generally mild, can bring cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails in Collbató?
A: Collbató village itself has several cafes and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your hike. Additionally, the Montserrat Monastery complex, accessible via some trails, also offers dining options and facilities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Collbató?
A: The hiking trails around Collbató are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique rock formations of Montserrat, the stunning panoramic views, and the diverse terrain that offers options for all skill levels.
tips: The Camino de San Juan through the Fuente Seca begins on the Camino de las Baterias, just after passing the Fuente Seca, and comes out on the Camino de las Ermitas, below the cave where the hermitages of San Juan and San Onofre used to be. It is a narrow and steep path that goes up the eastern slope of the stream that comes down from the hermitage of Santa Caterina, also known as the Torrente de la Fuente Seca. It has a drop of 585 meters and a length of 4.6 km. It is a very popular route with hikers, climbers and mountain lovers.

This crossroads is known as the Pla dels Soldats or Pla de Sant Miquel (in the first case it is known by this name because it was a French military campsite in the French War (1808-1814).
There are three possibilities to continue:

The path on the right goes along the Camino de las Ermitas in the direction of Pla de Taràntules where the upper station of Sant Joan is installed.
The road that continues in a straight line is the Camino de las Costas or the Camino de las Baterias in the direction of Collbató.
The path that turns to the left is the Camino de la Cuadra de San Miguel that goes down towards the Coll de la Fita or converges with the Camino de la Cueva Santa and in Collbató.
From this vantage point you get the famous postcard motif of the Monastery and the mountains of Montserrat.
One of the most beautiful places in Catalonia. Trekking, hiking, mountain biking, climbing, a little bit of everything. To enjoy nature alone or accompanied. Including to charge energy spiritually.

Very slippery in places due to loose scree and small round stones. Rather less suitable for small children and physically handicapped. Surefootedness required! But fantastic views over the landscape. GR96 and GR5 split halfway, but rejoin at the end up to the town of Montserrat cable car valley station. The mountain railway can also be booked "one way" and planned as a starting point and parking space.

The Santa Cova sanctuary is accessed by a path called Camí de la Santa Cova. Along the camí there is a series of sculptures dedicated to the rosary and the 15 mysteries of the Virgin, including works by Antoni Gaudí, Josep Puig i Cadafalch and other artists. The Santa Cova de Montserrat is where the Virgin of Montserrat was hidden during the Muslim invasions. It was later discovered by shepherds in the year 880. Its discovery made Montserrat a pilgrimage destination and led to the founding of Montserrat Abbey.

This hermitage is surrounded by a spectacular environment where the incredible rock formations of the Montserrat natural park stand out. It has picnic areas and a beautiful viewpoint from which you can see the mountains.

The Santo Corneli church has been documented since the 11th century. It seems that it was renovated during the sixteenth century or the first half of the seventeenth, although this must have been partial, since between the end of the eighteenth century and the beginning of the nineteenth the building was completely renovated, building it a new plant. It is then that the relief must have been partitioned, which appeared under the rose window on the façade during a restoration in 1985

faqs.question: Where does the Montserrat Monastery and Santa Cova Loop from Collbató trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends in Collbató. You'll find parking available in the town, which serves as a convenient base for this hike. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied and demanding. You'll encounter everything from cobbled paths to steep, eroded sections. The path descending past Santa Cova towards Collbató, for instance, can be very steep and initially descends over conglomerate rock. Good sure-footedness is essential.

faqs.question: Is this hike suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is a difficult hike requiring very good fitness and sure-footedness. With significant elevation gain and loss over its 13.8-kilometer length, it's best suited for experienced hikers.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in natural beauty and cultural landmarks. You'll pass by the Font Seca Trail, the Sant Miquel Hermitage, and the iconic Montserrat Monastery. A highlight is the Holy Cave of Montserrat (Santa Cova), reached via the 'camí del Rosari' lined with impressive sculptures. You'll also see the La Salut Hermitage of Collbató and the Church of Sant Corneli de Collbató.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: Expect breathtaking panoramic views throughout the hike. You'll see the unique, jagged rock formations of the Montserrat natural park, the Llobregat Valley, and distant natural spaces like Puigventós and Sant Llorenç del Munt i la serra de l'Obac. The View of the Llobregat Valley from Montserrat is particularly stunning.

faqs.question: Is the Montserrat Monastery and Santa Cova Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in Montserrat Natural Park, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially around the monastery and other populated areas. Given the challenging terrain with steep and eroded sections, ensure your dog is fit enough for the hike.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in Montserrat Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the trails within Montserrat Natural Park, including this loop. However, there might be fees for parking or for entering specific attractions like the Salnitre Cave near Collbató.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times to hike this trail. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making the steep sections more challenging, while winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially icy conditions at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, you'll find amenities, including cafes and restaurants, around the Montserrat Monastery complex. Collbató, your starting and ending point, also offers options for refreshments and meals.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other trails. Notable sections include parts of the Camí vell de Collbató a Montserrat, Camí de la Santa Cova, and Camí de Sant Miquel. It also briefly touches on longer routes like El Camí Metropolità: de Montserrat a Barcelona and Sender del Mil·lenari de Montserrat.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging hike?
faqs.answer: Given the difficulty and varied terrain, you should bring plenty of water, sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing. Trekking poles can be very helpful for the steep ascents and descents. Snacks and a first-aid kit are also recommended.
